mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, reduces economic uncertainty and allows for lower discount rates on future corporate earnings, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation generally supports equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is between 1% and 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to a decline in corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over any 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the Rank IC is consistently negative during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand supported by favorable monetary conditions, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.1%) when both conditions for strong demand and accommodative monetary policy are met.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, as indicated by a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), higher borrowing costs and reduced liquidity increase the discount rate for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the Rank IC is consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating robust future economic activity and demand, which typically translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.05%) in > 50% of periods where housing starts are robust and above the trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and broader supply-side contraction, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from declining industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and strong job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat (mean return < 0.1%) during periods of strong job growth as defined by the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and slowing economic growth (GDPC1) creates a stagflationary environment, which erodes corporate margins and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3% AND GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth < 1%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during periods of high inflation and low growth, or the Rank IC is consistently positive.
